paper_assessments.experimental_system: non-human (mouse); primary CD8+ T cells; cytotoxic effector/naive CD8+ T cells
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: JunB deficiency enhances the expression of co-inhibitory receptors, including programmed cell death 1 (PD-1) and TIM3
paper_assessments.rationale: Junb knockout perturbation increases co-inhibitory receptor (PD-1/TIM3) expression on activated CD8+ T cells, linking JunB loss to a checkpoint phenotype, though attenuated TCR signalling function itself is not directly measured; JunB is induced by TCR signal strength.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: non-human (mouse) and in vitro; non-immune vascular smooth muscle cells
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: EGCG effectively suppresses CKD-associated MAC by inhibiting the activity of JunB
paper_assessments.rationale: Concerns JunB in vascular smooth muscle cell osteogenic differentiation; no T cell or TCR relevance.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: MEK inhibition downregulated the JunB-driven gene profiles associated with exhaustion, differentiation, anergy
paper_assessments.rationale: Perturbational Cut-and-Tag plus JunB overexpression rescue implicate JunB in driving CAR-T exhaustion/anergy programs, but readout is CAR (not TCR) signalling and impaired TCR function is not explicitly demonstrated.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: literature review; immune cells including T cells
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: JunB has great regulatory functions in innate and adaptive immune responses
paper_assessments.rationale: Review of JunB in immunity and cancer; no primary TCR-inhibition data and a review cannot support strong/moderate tiers.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: non-human (mouse); primary bone marrow-derived dendritic cells (non-T immune)
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: JunB is required for inducing proinflammatory cytokines, partnering with NF-κB
paper_assessments.rationale: JunB drives proinflammatory cytokines in dendritic cells; not a T cell or TCR-inhibition system.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: literature review; urological cancer cells (non-immune focus)
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: JUNB and JUND are credited with both tumor-suppressing and oncogenic roles
paper_assessments.rationale: Review of JUNB/JUND in urological cancers; no T cell TCR-inhibition content.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: non-human (mouse); primary CD4+ T helper cells (Th1/Th2/Th17)
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: JunB serves a critical function in clonal expansion of diverse T helper cells by inhibiting their apoptosis
paper_assessments.rationale: JunB supports T helper survival/clonal expansion; this is pro-activation/survival biology, not an inhibitory TCR phenotype.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: human/mouse triple-negative breast cancer (tumor) cells; JunB acts in tumor cells
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: the NAT10-ac4C-JunB-LDHA pathway increases glycolysis levels and creates an immunosuppressive tumor microenvironment
paper_assessments.rationale: JunB in tumor cells promotes an immunosuppressive microenvironment via glycolysis; effect on T cells is indirect and not TCR-signalling inhibition.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: non-human (mouse); primary CD4+ T cells; Th17 subset
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: JunB promotes Th17 cell programs while repressing alternative CD4+ T-cell fates
paper_assessments.rationale: JunB governs Th17 lineage identity/differentiation, which is lineage specification rather than an inhibitory TCR phenotype.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: Lower JUNB expression was associated with a higher risk of prostatic adenocarcinoma progression
paper_assessments.rationale: Bioinformatic/histopathological prognostic association in prostate cancer; no T cell or TCR-inhibition evidence.

parsed.tier_rationale: Two primary studies materially link JunB to inhibitory T cell phenotypes: JunB deficiency raises co-inhibitory receptors PD-1/TIM3 in mouse CD8+ T cells (PMC11884676), and JunB perturbation drives CAR-T exhaustion/anergy gene programs (PMC11496645). Both provide relevant but incomplete evidence: neither directly demonstrates altered proximal TCR signalling, one is checkpoint-receptor expression only and the other is CAR (not TCR) signalling without explicit impaired-TCR-function readout. This meets moderate but not the strong requirement of direct causal TCR inhibition with a functional readout; between tiers, the lower tier applies.
